quality.rationale: 多步四动量守恒与代数推导+标准高能散射模型+题干解答齐全

origin.question: Problem 2.7. A particle of rest mass $m$ collides elastically with a stationary particle of equal mass. The incident particle has kinetic energy $T_0$ . What is its kinetic energy after the collision, if the scattering angle is $\theta$ ?## SolutionSolution 2.7. Let subscripts 0 and 1 denote respectively the initially moving, and the initially stationary particle. Balancing 4-momentum before and after the collision, we have$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P}_{0} + \mathbf {P}_{1} = \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}} + \mathbf {P}_{1^{\prime}} \\ (\mathbf {P}_{0} + \mathbf {P}_{1} - \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}})^{2} = \mathbf {P}_{1}^{2},\\ - 3 \mathrm {m}^{2} + 2 \mathbf {P}_{0} \cdot \mathbf {P}_{1} - 2 \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}} \cdot (\mathbf {P}_{0} + \mathbf {P}_{1}) = - \mathrm {m}^{2}. \\ \end{array}$$Now we put in$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P}_{0} = (\mathrm {E}, \underline {{\mathbf {P}}}), \quad \mathrm {E} = \mathrm {m} + \mathrm {T}_{0} \\ \mathbf {P}_{1} = (\mathrm {m}, \underline {{0}}), \quad \mathbf {E}^{\prime} = \mathrm {m} + \mathbf {T}^{\prime} \\ \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}} = \left(\mathrm {E}^{\prime}, \underline {{\mathrm {P}}}^{\prime}\right), \quad \underline {{\mathrm {P}}} \cdot \underline {{\mathrm {P}}}^{\prime} = \mathrm {P P}^{\prime} \cos \theta , \\ \end{array}$$to find$$- \mathrm {m}^{2} - \mathrm {E m} + \mathrm {E}^{\prime} (\mathrm {E} + \mathrm {m}) - \mathrm {P P}^{\prime} \cos \theta = 0$$i.e.$$\left(E^{2} - m^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left(E^{\prime 2} - m^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \cos \theta = \left(E^{\prime} - m\right) (E + m).$$Squaring and factoring the difference of squares, then gives the kinetic energy of the scattered particle:$$\begin{array}{l} (\mathrm {E} - \mathrm {m}) (\mathrm {E}^{\prime} + \mathrm {m}) \cos^{2} \theta = (\mathrm {E}^{\prime} - \mathrm {m}) (\mathrm {E} + \mathrm {m}) \\ \mathrm {T}_{0} \left(\mathrm {T}^{\prime} + 2 \mathrm {m}\right) \cos^{2} \theta = \mathrm {T}^{\prime} \left(\mathrm {T}_{0} + 2 \mathrm {m}\right) \\ \mathrm {T}^{\prime} \left(- \mathrm {T}_{0} \cos^{2} \theta + \mathrm {T}_{0} + 2 \mathrm {m}\right) = 2 \mathrm {m} \mathrm {T}_{0} \cos^{2} \theta \\ T^{\prime} = \frac {2 m T_{0} \cos^{2} \theta}{2 m + T_{0} \sin^{2} \theta}. \\ \end{array}$$
origin.solution: Solution 2.7. Let subscripts 0 and 1 denote respectively the initially moving, and the initially stationary particle. Balancing 4-momentum before and after the collision, we have$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P}_{0} + \mathbf {P}_{1} = \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}} + \mathbf {P}_{1^{\prime}} \\ (\mathbf {P}_{0} + \mathbf {P}_{1} - \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}})^{2} = \mathbf {P}_{1}^{2},\\ - 3 \mathrm {m}^{2} + 2 \mathbf {P}_{0} \cdot \mathbf {P}_{1} - 2 \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}} \cdot (\mathbf {P}_{0} + \mathbf {P}_{1}) = - \mathrm {m}^{2}. \\ \end{array}$$Now we put in$$\begin{array}{l} \mathbf {P}_{0} = (\mathrm {E}, \underline {{\mathbf {P}}}), \quad \mathrm {E} = \mathrm {m} + \mathrm {T}_{0} \\ \mathbf {P}_{1} = (\mathrm {m}, \underline {{0}}), \quad \mathbf {E}^{\prime} = \mathrm {m} + \mathbf {T}^{\prime} \\ \mathbf {P}_{0^{\prime}} = \left(\mathrm {E}^{\prime}, \underline {{\mathrm {P}}}^{\prime}\right), \quad \underline {{\mathrm {P}}} \cdot \underline {{\mathrm {P}}}^{\prime} = \mathrm {P P}^{\prime} \cos \theta , \\ \end{array}$$to find$$- \mathrm {m}^{2} - \mathrm {E m} + \mathrm {E}^{\prime} (\mathrm {E} + \mathrm {m}) - \mathrm {P P}^{\prime} \cos \theta = 0$$i.e.$$\left(E^{2} - m^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left(E^{\prime 2} - m^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \cos \theta = \left(E^{\prime} - m\right) (E + m).$$Squaring and factoring the difference of squares, then gives the kinetic energy of the scattered particle:$$\begin{array}{l} (\mathrm {E} - \mathrm {m}) (\mathrm {E}^{\prime} + \mathrm {m}) \cos^{2} \theta = (\mathrm {E}^{\prime} - \mathrm {m}) (\mathrm {E} + \mathrm {m}) \\ \mathrm {T}_{0} \left(\mathrm {T}^{\prime} + 2 \mathrm {m}\right) \cos^{2} \theta = \mathrm {T}^{\prime} \left(\mathrm {T}_{0} + 2 \mathrm {m}\right) \\ \mathrm {T}^{\prime} \left(- \mathrm {T}_{0} \cos^{2} \theta + \mathrm {T}_{0} + 2 \mathrm {m}\right) = 2 \mathrm {m} \mathrm {T}_{0} \cos^{2} \theta \\ T^{\prime} = \frac {2 m T_{0} \cos^{2} \theta}{2 m + T_{0} \sin^{2} \theta}. \\ \end{array}$$
